purpose: Frontend, backend, standards, and infrastructure technology stacks for modern web development. Documents map technology landscape, teaching sequence, and industry standards to employable competencies.

terminal_objective.prerequisite: language-fundamentals; programming-paradigms (functional + declarative required for React)
terminal_objective.concept: Frontend stack is HTML5 + CSS3 + TypeScript + React 18 + Vite; accessibility (WCAG AA) is a non-negotiable quality gate; teaching sequence is HTML → CSS → Vanilla JS → Async JS → React → TypeScript.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Build and deploy a TypeScript + React SPA with semantic HTML, responsive CSS layout, async data fetching, component state, keyboard accessibility, and a Vite production build.
terminal_objective.market_value: Critical — React + TypeScript required in ~67% of frontend job postings (2024–2026); accessibility compliance is a legal requirement in many jurisdictions.

educational_assets.title: Semantic HTML Accessibility Audit Lab
educational_assets.deliverable: Audit a provided HTML page for semantic structure violations and WCAG AA failures; produce a corrected version and a written accessibility report

educational_assets.title: CSS Layout Implementation Lab
educational_assets.deliverable: Implement 5 layout challenges using CSS Flexbox and Grid (no frameworks); each must be responsive and pass a provided visual regression test

educational_assets.title: TypeScript + React Data Dashboard
educational_assets.deliverable: Build a React + TypeScript SPA that fetches, displays, and filters data from a public API; includes typed components, custom hooks, error/loading states, and responsive layout

educational_assets.title: Async JavaScript and Fetch API Lab
educational_assets.deliverable: Implement three data-fetching patterns (callback, promise chain, async/await) for the same endpoint; identify error handling gaps in each

educational_assets.title: React Hooks Mental Model Quiz
educational_assets.deliverable: 15 questions on useState, useEffect, useRef, useCallback — including dependency array reasoning, stale closure identification, and render cycle prediction

terminal_objective.prerequisite: language-fundamentals; programming-paradigms (event-driven); frontend-stack for full-stack context
terminal_objective.concept: Backend stack (Node.js + Express + PostgreSQL + Prisma + JWT) provides the server runtime, REST API surface, relational data persistence, and authentication; security is foundational, not advanced.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Build a REST API with authenticated endpoints (JWT + bcrypt), parameterized queries via Prisma, zod input validation, structured error responses, and environment variable management.
terminal_objective.market_value: Critical — Node.js backend is the most common entry-level stack (2024–2026); security deficiencies are the most common reason junior devs are rejected in code review.

educational_assets.title: Express Middleware Pipeline Lab
educational_assets.deliverable: Build a middleware chain handling: JSON parsing, request logging, JWT authentication, role-based authorization, and structured error responses — in the correct order

educational_assets.title: SQL Injection Prevention Lab
educational_assets.deliverable: Identify SQL injection vulnerabilities in provided Express routes; replace all with parameterized Prisma queries; write a test that proves the fix

educational_assets.title: Full REST API with Auth
educational_assets.deliverable: Build a complete REST API (users, authentication, one resource); JWT auth with bcrypt passwords; input validation; correct HTTP status codes; Prisma + PostgreSQL; automated tests

educational_assets.title: HTTP Status Code Selection Exercise
educational_assets.deliverable: 20 API scenario descriptions — select the correct HTTP status code and explain the reasoning for each

educational_assets.title: REST API Design Conventions Quiz
educational_assets.deliverable: 25 questions on resource naming, HTTP method selection, query parameters vs path parameters, versioning, and idempotency

terminal_objective.concept: Web standards (WHATWG HTML, W3C CSS, ECMA-262, IETF HTTP, WCAG 2.1) define the interoperable behavior of all web technologies; standards literacy enables debugging across browser inconsistencies and compliance with legal accessibility requirements.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Diagnose a cross-browser CSS bug using the spec; write a WCAG AA audit against five success criteria; explain HTTP/2 multiplexing benefits in a performance engineering context.
terminal_objective.market_value: Medium-High — WCAG AA compliance is legally required in the EU, Canada, and increasingly the US; HTTP/2–3 knowledge required for performance engineering roles.

educational_assets.title: WCAG AA Audit Exercise
educational_assets.deliverable: Audit a provided webpage against WCAG 2.1 AA criteria using only DevTools and the spec; document each finding with success criterion number, failure description, and remediation

educational_assets.title: Standards Body Attribution Quiz
educational_assets.deliverable: Match 20 web technologies to their governing standards body; identify the correct specification document for each

educational_assets.title: HTTP Version Comparison Analysis
educational_assets.deliverable: Using browser DevTools Network tab, compare HTTP/1.1 vs HTTP/2 waterfall for the same page; quantify the performance difference; explain the mechanism

educational_assets.title: Accessibility Remediation Case Study
educational_assets.deliverable: Given a design mockup and existing HTML, produce a compliant version meeting WCAG 2.1 AA; justify every structural change with spec citations

terminal_objective.prerequisite: backend-stack; frontend-stack build tooling; basic terminal / language-fundamentals Tier 0
terminal_objective.concept: Web infrastructure (static hosting, application platforms, DNS, HTTPS, Docker, CI/CD) makes applications available to users; environment variable management and secrets hygiene are security-critical constraints enforced at this layer.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Configure a GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow; Dockerize a Node.js + PostgreSQL app with Docker Compose; map a custom domain with DNS records and HTTPS.
terminal_objective.market_value: High and rising — Docker + CI/CD is now expected at junior level; infrastructure-as-code literacy is in ~45% of full-stack job postings (2025–2026).

educational_assets.title: Dockerize a Node.js Application
educational_assets.deliverable: Write a production-quality Dockerfile for a provided Express API; create a docker-compose.yml with the app + PostgreSQL; verify the app runs and DB migrations execute

educational_assets.title: GitHub Actions CI Pipeline
educational_assets.deliverable: Configure a GitHub Actions workflow that runs lint, type-check, and test on every PR; blocks merge on failure; deploys to Render on main branch push

educational_assets.title: DNS Record Configuration Exercise
educational_assets.deliverable: Configure A, CNAME, and TXT records for a provided domain scenario using a DNS provider dashboard; verify propagation; explain each record's purpose

educational_assets.title: Full Deployment Pipeline Project
educational_assets.deliverable: Deploy a full-stack application end-to-end: Docker Compose locally, CI/CD via GitHub Actions, backend on Render, frontend on Vercel, custom domain with HTTPS, environment variables managed via platform secrets
